divergence_notes: Polarity/wrapper: stated as answer(False) ↔ P where P = (∀ A density-zero, ∃ B, A ⊆ B+B ∧ count = o(√N)) — FC house style for a disproved yes/no question (cf. FC 263.parts.ii). jayyhk's hosted erdos_333 is the direct negation ¬P; plby has NO erdos-named endpoint at all — its final main_obstruction is the conjunction (specific counterexample A has density zero) ∧ ¬∃ B …, which is strictly the witness form of ¬P. Hosted-vs-hosted: jayyhk wraps to ¬P, plby stops at the counterexample pair.

Density zero: we use FC's Set.HasDensity 0 (Tendsto of partialDensity = ncard over Set.Iio N divided by N); both hosted use Tendsto ((Finset.Icc 0 N).filter (· ∈ A)).card / N → 0. Iio N vs Icc 0 N counts differ by at most one element — the same natural-density-zero notion, not syntactically equal.

Counting range: the text says |B ∩ {1,…,N}|, so we count (Finset.Icc 1 N).filter (· ∈ B) (FC 32 house idiom); both hosted count over Finset.Icc 0 N (they include 0). Differs by ≤ 1, immaterial to o(√N); we follow the text.

Little-o: we formalize '= o(N^{1/2})' as Asymptotics.IsLittleO (=o[atTop] fun N => Real.sqrt N), the direct reading of the text and the FC idiom (cf. FC 32); both hosted use the equivalent Tendsto (count/√N) atTop (nhds 0). The text's 'for all large N' is absorbed into the atTop filter.

Sumset: A ⊆ B + B with Set pointwise addition (open scoped Pointwise); both hosted spell it out as A ⊆ {x | ∃ b b', b ∈ B ∧ b' ∈ B ∧ x = b + b'} — the same set (Set.mem_add is ∃ b ∈ B, ∃ b' ∈ B, b + b' = x; equation orientation flipped, propositionally equal).
B is an unconstrained Set ℕ (in particular not required to be a subset of A or infinite), matching both hosted and the text's bare 'a B'.
ams_rationale: 11 = number theory (additive bases: representing sets as B+B with thin B, Erdős–Newman).
